zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de Ros於大約1277年出生在英國越可爾英格曼東普時，她的父親Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp36歲，母親Eustacia Fitzhugh28歲。她於1273年與Marmaduke de Thweng在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1309年在英國越可爾吉爾頓享年33歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾克利夫蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel de Ros于大约1277年出生在英国越可爾英格曼東普时，她的父亲Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp36岁，母亲Eustacia Fitzhugh28岁。她于1273年与Marmaduke de Thweng在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1309年在英国越可爾吉爾頓享年33岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾克利夫蘭。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.
Swedish: ornamental name from ros ‘rose’.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de Ros was born about 1277, in Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp, was 36 and her mother, Eustacia Fitzhugh, was 28. She married Marmaduke de Thweng in 1273, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1309, in Kilton, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 33, and was buried in Cleveland, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de Ros est née environ 1277, à Ingmanthorpe, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Eustacia Fitzhugh, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Marmaduke de Thweng en 1273, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1309, à Kilton, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 33 ans, et a été enterrée à Cleveland, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel de Ros um 1277 in Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp 36 und ihre Mutter Eustacia Fitzhugh 28. Sie hat 1273 in Yorkshire, England Marmaduke de Thweng geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1309 im Alter von 33 Jahren in Kilton, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Cleveland, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Ros è nata il circa 1277 nel Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Eustacia Fitzhugh, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Marmaduke de Thweng nel 1273, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1309, nel Kilton, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 33 anni, e fu sepolta a Cleveland, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de Rosが約1277年にイギリスヨークシュアイアイングマンソルペで生まれた時，父Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorpは36歳，母Eustacia Fitzhughは28歳であった。1273年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでMarmaduke de Thwengと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1309年にイギリスヨークシュアイアキルトンで33歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアクレベランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Ros(이)가 대략 1277년 영국 요르크실르 잉먼소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eustacia Fitzhugh(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1273년 영국 요르크실르에서 Marmaduke de Thweng(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1309년 영국 요르크실르 킬턴에서 33세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 클레벨랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Ros nasceu aproximadamente 1277, em Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Eustacia Fitzhugh, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Marmaduke de Thweng em 1273, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1309, em Kilton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 33 anos, e foi sepultada em Cleveland, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de Ros родилась около 1277 г. в местности ингмантаурп, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Eustacia Fitzhugh, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Marmaduke de Thweng в 1273 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1309 г. в местности килтон, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 33 лет и была похоронена в местности кливеланд, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel de Ros nació aproximadamente 1277, en Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Eustacia Fitzhugh, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Marmaduke de Thweng en 1273, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1309, en Kilton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 33 años, y fue sepultada en Cleveland, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
